calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs On Appeal
Off Calendar Oct-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Tax Costs On Appeal:
Respondents and Defendants State Bar of California and J. Robert McPhail's motion to tax costs on appeal is granted in the amount of $286.80. Petitioner therefore is entitled to recover $2,004.20 in costs on appeal.

As the prevailing party on appeal, Petitioner is entitled to "only" the costs specified in California Rule of Court 8.278. (Cal. R. Ct. 8.278(d).) Petitioner is not entitled to recover $256.30 in expenses incurred in taking Minimum Continuing Legal Education classes, which petitioner incorrectly categorized as a "premium on any surety bond on appeal." The court is unpersuaded by petitioner's argument that these costs constitute the "functional equivalent" of a bond premium, an argument which is inconsistent with the plain language of the Rule and unsupported by any pertinent authority. Petitioner is entitled to recover $337.50 for hourly fees he paid for word processing to format and electronically bookmark his respondent's brief, which are properly considered costs of printing and reproducing the brief. (Cal. R. Ct. 8.278(d)(1)(E); see Lubetzky v. Friedman (1988) 199 Cal.App.3d 1350, 1356-1357 [costs of producing briefs include the cost of "preparing a brief on a word processor"].) Finally, petitioner concedes that the $20 cost of obtaining an audio recording of the oral argument before the Court of Appeal is not a permitted cost on appeal. The court rejects Respondents' contention that certain filing fees paid by petitioner were not reasonably incurred, with the exception of the $10.50 paid for the cost of filing his letter requesting delivery of an audio recording of the oral argument, which as noted is not recoverable. (See Muller v. Reagh (1959) 170 Cal.App.2d 151, 153 [court has discretion to disallow recovery of expense incurred solely for the incurring party's convenience].)

Counsel for the respondents is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER PHILIP OBBARD Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate is a duplicate entry.

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar May-23-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Philip B. Obbard, Tel. No. 1-510-380-5621, Plaintiff appearing in npro per;
Atty. Sean T. Strauss of the State Bar of California, Tel. No. 1-415-538-2369 appearing for the Respondent.

RULING - Judge Harold Kahn, who is hearing this matter, discloses that he knows, has worked with and is friends with many attorneys who are now or in the past have been employed by the San Francisco Superior Court, but believes that he is and can be fair and impartial to both sides of this case. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner/plaintiff Philip Obbard's petition for writ of mandate is granted and his procedurally invalid request for relief on his complaint for declaratory relief is off calendar as moot. (See word Minutes for details). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
